records.chapter_title: ASI Is a Stack, Not a Model
records.formal_target: A layer without external-action authority can only produce an action through an authorized handoff.

records.chapter_title: ASI Is a Stack, Not a Model
records.formal_target: A handoff that exceeds the caller authority ceiling is rejected.

records.chapter_title: ASI Is a Stack, Not a Model
records.formal_target: Modeled layer-contract admission routes missing layer identity, lifecycle state, owner, responsibility, input artifacts, output artifacts, authority ceilings, handoff protocols, invariants, failure modes, evidence gates, external-action authority or handoff boundaries, source mappings, support-state boundaries, evidence-transition gaps, and non-claim-boundary gaps to explicit outcomes.

records.chapter_title: The Efficient ASI Hypothesis
records.formal_target: A route is minimum viable only when no lower-cost authorized route satisfies the required quality predicate.

records.chapter_title: The Efficient ASI Hypothesis
records.formal_target: A routed or compressed result with open obligations cannot be promoted without a residual record.

records.chapter_title: The Efficient ASI Hypothesis
records.formal_target: The synthetic efficiency route-search probe summary records two valid traces, six expected-invalid controls, fourteen checked candidate routes, minimum verified route selection, rejection of cheap failed quality, hidden residual, authority bypass, and compression-utility overclaim controls, hidden-cost class audit coverage, and no route-search-completeness, measured-efficiency, or support-state-promotion claim.

records.chapter_title: System Boundaries and Authority

records.formal_target: A missing grant blocks execution rather than becoming default authorization.

records.formal_target: Modeled failure recurrence and receipt review routes missing failure class, boundary, receipt, owner, containment, residual, learning path, normalization guard, review escalation, quarantine, evidence-transition, and non-claim-boundary records to explicit outcomes.

records.chapter_title: Human Intent as a Formal Input

records.formal_target: The benchmark anti-Goodhart fixture bridge mirrors 2 valid fixtures, 5 expected-invalid controls, promotion-ready, regression-floor, missing-checks, blocked-ratchet policy, reward-as-truth, saturated-promotion, release-approval, no-support-promotion, and non-claim-boundary facts.

records.chapter_title: Policy Optimization and Learning from Feedback
records.formal_target: An admitted policy update records target layer, reward signal, update constraint, evaluation refs, governance gates, and rollback plan.

records.chapter_title: Policy Optimization and Learning from Feedback
records.formal_target: A policy update with unverified reward or missing governance gate cannot be promoted.

records.chapter_title: Policy Optimization and Learning from Feedback
records.formal_target: A policy-promotion route rejects or routes updates with inadmissible feedback, missing target evaluation, missing holdout or contamination checks, missing reward-hacking probes, governance or authority gaps, missing rollback, or regression/residual gaps before promotion.

records.chapter_title: Policy Optimization and Learning from Feedback
records.formal_target: The deterministic policy-update lease probe records six synthetic routing samples, five candidate policies, selected canary kept experimental, expected-invalid controls, rollback dry run, no support-state effect, and non-claim boundary.

records.chapter_title: Artifact Steward Agents and Living Project Governance
records.formal_target: A steward-managed work contract records objective, authority, allowed tools, forbidden tools, verification requirements, budget, and non-claims before dispatch.

records.chapter_title: Artifact Steward Agents and Living Project Governance
records.formal_target: A steward action that exceeds treasury policy, changes governance rules, or touches protected assets cannot execute without explicit approval evidence.

records.chapter_title: Artifact Steward Agents and Living Project Governance
records.formal_target: A stewarded release publication requires test, evidence, changelog, residual, and approval records.

records.chapter_title: Artifact Steward Agents and Living Project Governance
records.formal_target: When sunset criteria are met, ordinary work generation is blocked until a sunset review is opened.

records.chapter_title: Artifact Steward Agents and Living Project Governance
records.formal_target: A steward lifecycle route sends tainted unreviewed events to quarantine, sunset criteria without review to sunset review, autonomy escalation without charter approval to approval, and over-policy treasury spend to approval.

records.chapter_title: Artifact Steward Agents and Living Project Governance
records.formal_target: A steward contribution ledger keeps authorship, review, evidence, compensation, reputation, governance effect, and conflicts separated; collapsed governance scoring is rejected and support-state changes require evidence-transition records.

records.chapter_title: Artifact Steward Agents and Living Project Governance
records.formal_target: A steward federation contract requires scoped work contracts, bounded worker authority, tool/data/budget gates, external-spend approval, and evidence-bundle requirements before dispatch.

records.chapter_title: Integrated Reference Architecture
records.formal_target: An end-to-end trace contains required artifacts for each layer handoff.

records.chapter_title: Integrated Reference Architecture
records.formal_target: A trace with a missing governance gate cannot be marked valid.

records.chapter_title: Project Theseus as Report-First Implementation Reference
records.formal_target: An implementation-reference claim names the report, config, or tool surface and does not rely on dashboard prose alone.

records.chapter_title: Project Theseus as Report-First Implementation Reference
records.formal_target: A capability or self-evolution promotion is blocked when required gate reports are absent or failing.

records.chapter_title: Prototype Roadmap
records.formal_target: A roadmap phase can unlock a dependent phase only after acceptance gates pass.

records.chapter_title: Prototype Roadmap
records.formal_target: A phase milestone cannot promote a claim without evidence artifacts.

records.chapter_title: Living Book Methodology
records.formal_target: Every chapter in the manifest has outline proof targets and generated claim placeholders.

records.chapter_title: Living Book Methodology
records.formal_target: A structural update without regenerated scaffold/proof manifest is invalid.

records.chapter_title: Living Book Methodology
records.formal_target: A public-surface change packet requires validation, changelog, support-state, non-claim, derivative-boundary, and evidence-transition discipline.

records.chapter_title: Open Research Agenda and Bibliography Plan
records.formal_target: A source-derived claim requires a source note or equivalent ingested-source artifact.

records.chapter_title: Open Research Agenda and Bibliography Plan
records.formal_target: A new source cannot be assigned to a non-existent chapter id.
